steps.action: POST authenticated teams query for id/key/name
steps.expected: At least one team with populated id, key, and name fields
steps.observed: Returned one team with id 457f605c-82b1-444f-bbe8-21037d92f422, key INV, and name Involute.

assertions.title: Seed data — six workflow states exist

steps.action: POST authenticated teams query for nested state names
steps.expected: Default team exposes exactly Backlog, Ready, In Progress, In Review, Done, and Canceled
steps.observed: Returned exactly 6 workflow states for INV: Backlog, Ready, In Progress, In Review, Done, Canceled.

assertions.title: Seed data — at least 10 labels exist

steps.action: POST authenticated issueLabels query for label names
steps.expected: At least 10 labels including task, epic, spec, needs-clarification, blocked, agent-ready, Feature, Bug, Improvement, and spec-orch
steps.observed: Returned 10 labels: Bug, Feature, Improvement, agent-ready, blocked, epic, needs-clarification, spec, spec-orch, task.

assertions.title: Seed data — authenticated user identity resolves with isMe

steps.action: POST authenticated introspection query for Query and User fields plus issue(id) lookup with a placeholder UUID
steps.expected: A read-only query path should allow observing a real User node with isMe: true for the authenticated user
steps.observed: Introspection showed only Query.issue(id), Query.teams, and Query.issueLabels as root fields; User exposes isMe, but issue(id) requires an opaque ID and the placeholder lookup returned null.

assertions.issues: Blocked on the assigned read-only surface: there is no root query to enumerate issues or users, and issue(id) requires an undiscoverable opaque ID. Without mutating data or using out-of-band database/code knowledge, a real-surface query cannot reach a User instance to confirm isMe: true.

steps.action: Launch 5 authenticated teams queries in parallel with curl
steps.expected: All 5 requests return valid data within 5 seconds and the server remains healthy
steps.observed: All 5 requests returned HTTP 200 with the seeded INV/Involute team; aggregate wall-clock time was 0.015944 seconds.

blockers.description: VAL-FOUND-010 cannot be proven from the permitted read-only curl surface because no root query enumerates users or issues and issue(id) requires an opaque undiscoverable ID.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Used authenticated schema introspection to confirm the available root fields and tried a non-mutating issue(id) lookup; still no user node was reachable on-surface.

summary: Tested 5 assertions: 4 passed (VAL-FOUND-007, VAL-FOUND-008, VAL-FOUND-009, VAL-FOUND-012) and 1 was blocked (VAL-FOUND-010) because the allowed read-only API surface cannot reach a User node with isMe without an undiscoverable issue ID or a mutation.
